REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 210, DEFENDANT FRANK STONICH'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S ATTENDANCE AT INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL MEDICAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED. EXAMINATION SHALL PROMPTLY PROCEED REMOTELY WITH DR. GERSHWIN. PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER PRIOR TO THE HEARING TO AGREE ON A DATE. IF PARTIES CANNOT AGREE EXAMINATION SHALL PROCEED ON AUGUST 18, 2021 AT 9:00 A.M. IN ADDITION, THE COURT DENIES BOTH REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 BY EMAIL TO DEPARTMENT210@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: VEDICA PURI, CLERK: M. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT FRANK STONICH DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 26, 2020. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER ADVANCES SEVERAL DISTINCT AND INDEPENDENT ARGUMENTS SEEKING AN ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION BY NOT ADDRESSING THE ARGUMENT ADVANCED BY THE DEFENDANT IN SECTION D OF THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, APPEARS TO BE CONCEDING THE ISSUE. IF OPPOSITION WAS INTENTIONALLY OMITTED. THE COURT WILL SUSTAIN THE DEMURRER BASED ON DEFENDANT'S UNOPPOSED ARGUMENT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. IF PLAINTIFF INADVERTENTLY OMITTED OPPOSITION TO THIS ISSUE, PLAINTIFF SHALL (1) FILE A DECLARATION REGARDING THE OMISSION; AND (2) FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF ADDRESSING SECTION D OF DEFENDANT'S MOVING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, NOT TO EXCEED 3 PAGES. SUPPLEMENTAL FILINGS DESCRIBED ABOVE TO BE FILED AND SERVED NO LATER THAN 5:00 P.M. ON AUGUST 19, 2020. DEFENDANT MAY FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY, NOT TO EXCEED 3 PAGES, BY NOON ON AUGUST 24, 2020. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar Aug-30-2021 Continued To Nov-10-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Aug-11-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kristen Drake, Esq. 888-510-7511 of Tenant Law Group for Plaintiff.
James Patrick, Esq. 415-438-6625 of Lewis Brisbois Bisgard & Smith LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defense Argues. Plaintiff responds with 3 issues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT FRANK STONICH's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant moves for summary adjudication of three "issues." Issue No. 1: The first issue combines multiple causes of action purportedly subject to a 1-year statute of limitations. However, the MPAs relevant to this issue do not address each cause of action separately; no authority is cited for the proposition that each statutory violation is subject to a 1-year statute of limitations and no facts in the separate statement establish the date the statute of limitations started running for the purposes of CC 1950.5 (failure to return/account for the security deposit). Summary adjudication cannot be granted on this issue as framed. Issue No. 2: The second issue purports to address "remaining" causes of action, ostensibly, those that are not addressed in the first issue. However, the same causes of action that were the subject of the first issue are referenced in the separate statement under the second issue. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that all causes of action are based upon the same primary right, e.g., wrongful eviction vs. habitability vs. security deposit. As such, summary adjudication cannot be granted on the second issue as framed. Issue No. 3: The third issue is supported by one fact in the separate statement, which establishes that an unlawful detainer complaint was filed. The failure to cite any more facts or evidence in support of its request for a judgment as a matter of law is fatal. Motion for Summary Judgment is denied for the same reasons as stated above.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by email to Department210@sftc.org

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (210)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-19-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FRANK STONICH DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is continued to August 26, 2020. Defendant's demurrer advances several distinct and independent arguments seeking an order sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend. Plaintiff's opposition by not addressing the argument advanced by the Defendant in section D of the Points and Authorities, appears to be conceding the issue. If opposition was intentionally omitted. the Court will sustain the demurrer based on Defendant's unopposed argument without leave to amend. If Plaintiff inadvertently omitted opposition to this issue, Plaintiff shall (1) file a declaration regarding the omission; and (2) file a supplemental brief addressing section D of Defendant's moving Points and Authorities, not to exceed 3 pages. Supplemental filings described above to be filed and served no later than 5:00 p.m. on August 19, 2020. Defendant may file a supplemental reply, not to exceed 3 pages, by noon on August 24, 2020.
